name: Alissa Trollinger
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Deputy Associate Superintendent / State Director of Special Education
story_relevance_note: As Arizona's State Director of Special Education, Trollinger is the highest-ranking ADE official accountable for IDEA compliance and the implementation of restraint and seclusion guidance under ARS 15-105. Any data investigation showing restraint patterns at AZ K-12 schools lands on her desk first. She sets the policy floor for IEPs, behavior intervention plans, and FAPE protections statewide.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Arizona Part B, DMS Status Update" close-out letter from the U.S. Department of Education on March 1, 2023.

name: Angela Odom
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Assistant State Director of Special Education
story_relevance_note: Odom is the second-in-command at ADE Special Ed. She owns day-to-day execution of Arizona's IDEA monitoring, including the Results-Driven Accountability framework that should be flagging districts with high restraint counts. A restraint-data investigation gives her the documentation she'd need to escalate or audit specific PEAs.

name: Celeste Nameth
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Nameth is ADE's Lead Behavior Specialist, the single most relevant staff role to a restraint and seclusion data story. She advises districts on functional behavior assessments, positive behavior interventions, and de-escalation training that, when missing, drives restraint counts up. Her name belongs on any AZ K-12 restraint reform brief.
personalization_hook: Saw your MTBS Year 1 2025-2026 Readiness Flyer on Multi-Tier Behavior Supports Training for K, 12.

name: Lauren Friend
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Friend coordinates the intersection of behavior intervention and student mental health at ADE. Restraint events overwhelmingly happen to students with documented disabilities and trauma histories. She is the staffer who can speak to the pipeline from missed mental health support to physical restraint in AZ schools.

name: Christy Hegebush
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Federal data shows autistic students are restrained at rates several times their share of enrollment. Hegebush is ADE's named contact for autism and low-incidence disabilities, and she is the staff face of any AZ-specific dataset that breaks restraint counts down by disability category.
personalization_hook: Saw your contact role for the **2-Day STAR Comprehensive Workshop** on evidence-based autism practices with ADE/ESS and STAR Autism Support.

name: Jeffrey Studer
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Studer runs the IDEA state-complaint and due-process pipeline for Arizona. Restraint and seclusion incidents are a leading driver of formal complaints filed by parents. Aggregated restraint data lets him see which districts generate the most disputes and which restraint patterns precede formal complaints.
personalization_hook: Saw your State Complaint Investigative Report and Corrective Action on the AZ State School for the Deaf and Blind received November 14, 2024.

name: Nicole Barnette
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Barnette is ADE's Lead Complaint Investigator. She's the staffer who actually opens files when a parent alleges illegal restraint or seclusion. A statewide restraint dataset gives her a frame for every individual case she takes in, and shows which districts cluster.

name: Scott Dobkovsky
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Dobkovsky directs ADE's Program Support and Monitoring team, which audits district-level IDEA compliance. Restraint and seclusion compliance, including required parent notification and post-incident review, falls inside his monitoring scope. Aggregated AZ restraint data is the kind of evidence that drives PSM caseload prioritization.

name: Heidi Putnam
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Putnam co-directs ADE's Program Support and Monitoring with Dobkovsky. She owns the technical assistance side, working directly with districts that are out of compliance. A restraint data investigation is the kind of external pressure that gets PSM technical-assistance visits scheduled.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on the "School Year 2020-2021 Monitoring Updates" Special Education Directors' Check-In as Lead Specialist for Program Support and Monitoring.

name: Heather Dunphy
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Lead Education Program Specialist - Results Driven Accountability
story_relevance_note: Dunphy leads Arizona's Results-Driven Accountability work, the federal IDEA framework that should track significant disproportionality, including disproportionate restraint of Black, Latino, and disabled students. A statewide restraint dataset is a primary input to RDA decisions.
personalization_hook: Saw your presentation on the 2023 State.

name: Adam Zogut
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Data Analysis Specialist - Results Driven Accountability
story_relevance_note: Zogut is ADE's data analyst on the Results-Driven Accountability team. He's the staffer who actually pulls and crunches the numbers that ADE leadership uses to flag districts. A clean public dataset on restraint, with AZ-specific breakouts, is exactly the input his role is designed to ingest.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on "Arizona Preschool and K-12 Special Education Trend Data 2020-2024".

name: Sam Klein
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Klein directs ADE Special Projects from the Tucson office, which gives him the southern AZ lens on restraint and seclusion patterns. His portfolio spans assistive technology and secondary transition, both areas where restraint events disrupt students' IEPs and post-school outcomes.

name: Lisa Livesay
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Livesay leads ADE's secondary transition work, which is where restraint history follows students into post-school outcomes, including disciplinary alternative placements. Restraint counts at the high school level correlate with worse transition outcomes, a connection she's uniquely positioned to discuss.
personalization_hook: Saw your presentation on "Getting Ahead to Go Beyond: Secondary Transition Changes and Considerations" at the Special Education Advisory Panel meeting.

name: Stacy Riccio
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Director of Academic Achievement & Inclusive Practices
story_relevance_note: Riccio directs Inclusive Practices at ADE, the team that helps districts keep students with disabilities in general education classrooms. Restraint and seclusion data is a leading indicator of inclusion failure, since exclusion to seclusion rooms is the opposite of the LRE mandate her team enforces.

name: Aanya Metrakos
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Director of Early Childhood Special Education
story_relevance_note: Metrakos directs ADE's Early Childhood Special Education program. Pre-K and K-2 restraint events are particularly concerning because of developmental impact. She's the named staffer who would care most about restraint counts at the youngest grade bands.

name: Tracey Sridharan
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: Director of Professional Learning and Sustainability
story_relevance_note: Sridharan directs ADE's professional learning, the function that determines what de-escalation and restraint training AZ teachers and paraprofessionals receive. A restraint dataset showing patterns by district is the input that should drive her training calendar.

name: Stefanie Sharkey
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
role: AZ FIND Coordinator / Surrogate Parent Program Coordinator
story_relevance_note: Sharkey runs both AZ Find (Child Find identification) and the Surrogate Parent Program for AZ kids in foster care. Foster youth and Child Find late-identified students are both populations with elevated restraint risk. She's the named staffer for two of the most vulnerable subgroups in the data.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Surrogate Parent: When, Why, How" presentation from the Arizona Department of Education on surrogate parent roles and responsibilities.

name: Mary Keeney
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Keeney leads Related Services, which covers the OT, PT, speech, and counseling supports written into IEPs. Missing or under-delivered related services correlate with behavior crises that escalate to restraint. Her staff is the de facto early-warning system in the data.

name: Doug Cutler
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Cutler is ADE's Assistive Technology Specialist. AAC devices and other AT often prevent the communication frustration that triggers escalation. When AT is missing from an IEP and a student is restrained, that's an avoidable failure he can document.
personalization_hook: Saw your Bookshare ESS Presentation on January 18, 2024, highlighting accessible e-books for students with print disabilities.

name: Kim Rice
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Rice leads ADE's effort to recruit and retain special education teachers. Restraint events spike in classrooms with vacant or undertrained sped staff. The data set ties her workforce work directly to student safety outcomes.
personalization_hook: Saw your "ESS Presentation for OIE P and P" on Exceptional Student Services at the August 19, 2024 convening.

name: Colette Chapman
outlet: Arizona Department of Education
story_relevance_note: Chapman is the Associate Superintendent above ADE Special Ed. Restraint and seclusion patterns are one of the highest-political-risk topics in the agency, and a public AZ dataset reaches her for sign-off on response, especially around districts that draw repeat federal attention.

name: Tanya Rodriguez
outlet: Arizona Department of Education - Exceptional Student Services
story_relevance_note: Rodriguez leads Program Management at ADE Special Ed, the team that handles federal IDEA Part B grant administration and reporting. Districts must report restraint and seclusion data to her chain to keep grant money flowing. That makes her uniquely positioned on the data integrity question the AZ Law Now investigation raises.
personalization_hook: Saw your work on the **FY25 ESS Claims** for IDEA grant distribution and High-Cost Child Fund eligibility.
